request.description: Always 200 (generic anti-enumeration message), even if the email doesn't exist.
The token comes from the link received by email (Mailjet/Mailpit locally) — copy it manually into the reset_password_token variable before running this request.

formdata.description: Choose a PNG/JPG/WEBP file (< 3 MB) before running this request.

item.name: Add the customized product to the cart

exec: pm.test("Le panier contient au moins un article", () => pm.expect(data.items.length).to.be.above(0));

request.description: Always 405: an order can only be created via POST /api/payment/intent (a single order + payment + shipment transaction).

exec: pm.test('Status 201 ou 422 (rejet modération) ou 503 (IA indisponible)', () => pm.expect([201, 422, 503]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Real call to the OpenAI API (gpt-image-1), can take 20-40s. Requires the target product to have allow_ai_generation=true.

exec: pm.test('Répond (signature invalide attendue en dehors de Stripe)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(400));

request.description: Endpoint called ONLY by Stripe, never manually: requires a valid Stripe-Signature header computed with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. To test locally, use `stripe listen --forward-to localhost:8000/api/stripe/webhook` (Stripe CLI) instead of this request, which is only here to document the contract (400 expected without a valid signature).
Uses an account with the admin role. Fill in admin_email/admin_password in the collection variables (never real values in plaintext in a commit).
